pokemon_facts.fact: Cubone wears the skull of its dead mother. Its crying echoes inside the skull, making the sound even sadder.
Drifloon is known to carry away small children by grabbing their hands and floating off.
Yamask carries a mask that used to be its face when it was human.
Banette was originally a discarded doll that came to life seeking revenge on the child who threw it away.
Gengar hides in people's shadows and slowly drains their life force.
Hypno has a reputation for hypnotising children and leading them away.
Phantump are said to be the spirits of children who died lost in the forest.
Froslass freezes its victims and keeps them as decorations in its icy den.
Paras is slowly being taken over by the mushroom growing on its back.
Parasect is completely controlled by the mushroom parasite.
Magcargo's body temperature is about 18,000°F (10,000°C), hotter than the surface of the sun.
Slowpoke sometimes forgets what it was doing while already doing it.
Psyduck gets headaches so painful they unleash powerful psychic attacks.
Snorlax wakes up only to eat massive amounts of food before falling asleep again.
Wobbuffet attacks only when its trainer isn't looking.
Ditto can transform into anything, but sometimes it keeps Ditto's simple face.
Magikarp is so weak that it sometimes dies from jumping too high.
Yet Magikarp evolves into the terrifying dragon-like Gyarados.
Slaking is extremely powerful but spends most of its time loafing around.
Spinda has over 4 billion possible spot pattern combinations.
Arceus is said to have created the entire Pokémon universe.
Mew contains the DNA of every Pokémon.
Mewtwo was created through genetic manipulation of Mew.
Giratina was banished to the Distortion World for its violent nature.
Dialga controls time itself.
Palkia governs the fabric of space.
Rayquaza lives in the ozone layer and calms battles between Kyogre and Groudon.
Kyogre expands the oceans whenever it awakens.
Groudon expands the continents when it emerges.
Necrozma absorbs light and can plunge worlds into darkness.
Alakazam has an IQ said to exceed 5,000.
Kadabra was once rumored to resemble a psychic boy who turned into a Pokémon.
Machamp can throw 1,000 punches in two seconds.
Onix's body is made of giant boulders that become rounder as it tunnels underground.
Steelix is harder than diamond.
Dragonite can circle the globe in about 16 hours.
Charizard's flame goes out if it dies.
Blastoise's cannons are powerful enough to punch holes through steel.
Venusaur's giant flower absorbs sunlight for energy.
Pikachu stores electricity in its cheek pouches.
Raichu's electric attacks can reach 100,000 volts.
Jigglypuff sings lullabies that instantly put listeners to sleep.
Meowth is one of the few Pokémon known to speak human language.
Persian's elegant appearance hides a very vicious temper.
Farfetch'd fights using a leek like a sword.
Doduo has two brains, allowing it to react extremely quickly.
Dodrio has three heads that think independently.
Muk smells so bad that touching it can cause fainting.
Grimer was born from toxic sludge.
Koffing constantly leaks poisonous gas.
Weezing can mix different toxic gases to create stronger poisons.
Drowzee eats dreams.
Krabby can grow back lost pincers.
Kingler's oversized claw can crush shells easily.
Electrode often explodes with little warning.
Voltorb resembles Poké Balls to trick people.
Exeggcute are actually six independent beings working together.
Exeggutor's heads occasionally argue with each other.
Marowak fiercely protects its bone weapon.
Hitmonlee's legs can stretch like springs.
Hitmonchan's punches are said to move faster than the eye can see.
Lickitung can extend its tongue over two meters.
Chansey shares its nutritious eggs with injured people and Pokémon.
Kangaskhan babies almost never leave their mother's pouch.
Horsea shoots ink to escape predators.
Seadra's poisonous spines can cause fainting.
Goldeen is known as the 'Water Queen' due to its elegance.
Seaking can swim upstream powerful waterfalls.
Staryu regenerates its limbs quickly.
Starmie communicates using mysterious radio signals.
Mr. Mime creates invisible walls using psychic power.
Scyther moves so fast it appears to have multiple copies.
Jynx communicates through dance-like movements.
Electabuzz is often seen near power plants.
Magmar's body burns with internal fire.
Pinsir crushes prey with its massive horns.
Tauros becomes extremely aggressive when it spots red.
Lapras is gentle and intelligent but was once hunted nearly to extinction.
Porygon was created entirely from computer code.
Omanyte lived millions of years ago and was revived from fossils.
Kabutops slices prey apart with razor-sharp limbs.
Aerodactyl is an ancient predator revived from fossils.
Articuno freezes moisture in the air to create snow.
Zapdos generates powerful lightning storms.
Moltres ignites the sky with its fiery wings.
Dratini sheds its skin as it grows.
Dragonair can manipulate weather patterns.
Dragonite is kind-hearted despite its immense strength.
Mew is so playful it sometimes turns invisible.
Some Pokémon like Shedinja appear mysteriously when others evolve.
Shedinja's hollow body is said to steal the souls of anyone who looks into the hole on its back.
Gardevoir will create a black hole to protect its trainer.
Absol is often blamed for disasters even though it only appears to warn people.
Sharpedo can swim at speeds up to 75 mph.
Wailord is one of the largest Pokémon ever discovered.
Delibird delivers presents but sometimes they explode.
Sableye eats gemstones.
Tyranitar can alter landscapes with its power.
Lugia's wings can create storms strong enough to destroy houses.
Ho-Oh is said to grant eternal happiness to those who see it.
Shedinja appears mysteriously in a Poké Ball when Nincada evolves, even though no one ever sees where it came from.
Looking directly into the hole on Shedinja's back is said to steal your soul.
Gardevoir is so loyal it will distort space and create a miniature black hole to protect its trainer.
Gallade fights like a knight, using blades that extend from its elbows.
Beldum moves by firing magnetic pulses that repel the ground.
Metagross has four brains that work together, making it smarter than a supercomputer.
Salamence grew wings out of sheer determination to fly.
Bagon repeatedly jumps off cliffs trying to fly before it evolves.
Shuppet feeds on feelings of jealousy and revenge.
Duskull can pass through walls and is said to spirit away naughty children.
Dusclops absorbs anything into the hollow darkness of its body.
Dusknoir guides lost spirits to the afterlife.
Chandelure burns spirits instead of wax to keep its flames alive.
Litwick pretends to guide people in dark areas but secretly drains their life energy.
Lampent waits near hospitals to absorb the spirits of the recently deceased.
Spiritomb is made from 108 trapped spirits bound to an odd keystone.
Mimikyu wears a crude Pikachu disguise to avoid being shunned.
Anyone who sees Mimikyu's true form reportedly dies from shock.
Bewear hugs are so powerful they can break a person's spine.
Komala spends its entire life asleep while clinging to its log.
Komala never lets go of its log because it considers it a lifelong companion.
Sandygast was formed from sand possessed by grudges of the dead.
Palossand lures people close before dragging them into its sandy body.
Yveltal absorbs the life force of all living things when it dies.
Xerneas shares eternal life energy through its glowing antlers.
Zygarde monitors the balance of ecosystems across the world.
Some Zygarde forms are made from hundreds of tiny cells acting together.
Greninja can create water shurikens sharp enough to slice metal.
Froakie creates protective bubbles around its neck and back.
Delphox can peer into the future by staring into its flames.
Fennekin expels hot air from its ears to keep enemies away.
Chespin hardens the spikes on its head to block attacks.
Quilladin can tackle enemies with explosive force.
Chesnaught can block tank shells with its armored body.
Honedge was once a sword used by a human warrior.
Doublade moves by controlling its two blades telekinetically.
Aegislash chooses kings worthy of ruling.
Carbink are born deep underground under intense pressure.
Diancie is said to be a sudden mutation of Carbink.
Noivern can emit ultrasonic waves powerful enough to shatter rock.
Hawlucha fights with dramatic wrestling-style moves.
Talonflame dives at speeds exceeding 300 mph.
Flabébé rides on a flower it picks the moment it is born.
Floette cares for its flower with extreme devotion.
Florges controls entire gardens of flowers.
Skiddo rarely startles and calmly carries people on its back.
Gogoat can sense its rider's emotions through its horns.
Heliolisk absorbs sunlight to generate electricity.
Tyrunt is revived from ancient fossils of prehistoric Pokémon.
Tyrantrum was known as the king of ancient predators.
Amaura lived in ancient icy climates.
Aurorus can lower the surrounding temperature dramatically.
Sylveon uses ribbon-like feelers to calm hostile Pokémon.
Eevee has unstable DNA that allows it to evolve into many forms.
Umbreon glows with yellow rings when it releases poison.
Espeon can read air currents to predict opponents' movements.
Glaceon can instantly freeze moisture in the air.
Leafeon produces clean air around forests where it lives.
Vaporeon's body is mostly water.
Jolteon's fur generates static electricity constantly.
Flareon's internal flame sac can reach extremely high temperatures.
Umbreon evolved through exposure to moonlight.
Espeon evolved through strong friendship during the daytime.
Sylveon evolves when it bonds strongly with its trainer.
Rotom can possess household appliances.
Rotom's forms include a microwave, washing machine, fan, fridge, and lawn mower.
Porygon-Z was created after an experimental software update.
Genesect is an ancient Pokémon modified by Team Plasma.
Victini is said to guarantee victory to trainers who have it.
Zoroark can create massive illusions affecting entire groups of people.
Zorua disguises itself as humans or other Pokémon.
Hydreigon destroys anything that moves.
Deino is blind and bites anything nearby.
Larvesta eventually evolves into the sun-like Volcarona.
Volcarona once replaced the sun during a volcanic winter.
Krookodile uses its powerful jaws to crush prey.
Sandile hides under desert sand with only its eyes exposed.
Sigilyph patrols ancient ruins.
Golurk can fly using rocket-like energy from its legs.
Golett was built by an ancient civilization.
Reuniclus fights using psychic arms made of energy.
Duosion are two brains competing inside one body.
Solosis communicates through telepathy.
Cofagrigus traps grave robbers inside its body.
Yamask constantly remembers its past human life.
Trubbish loves dirty places filled with garbage.
Garbodor spreads toxic waste when angered.
Klink are two gears that spin together constantly.
Klinklang spins its gears to generate energy.
Excadrill can drill through bedrock.
Drilbur tunnels underground at incredible speeds.
Audino can hear heartbeats and detect illness.
Lilligant dances gracefully to confuse opponents.
Petilil absorbs sunlight for growth.
Whimsicott sneaks into homes through tiny cracks.
Cottonee rides the wind like a tiny cotton puff.
Maractus dances to create rhythmic sounds.
Crustle carries a massive stone shell on its back.
Dwebble fights over the best rocks to use as homes.
The Pokémon world contains over a thousand different species discovered so far.
Unown are shaped like letters of the alphabet and seem to communicate through mysterious psychic signals.
Large groups of Unown can reportedly bend reality itself.
Celebi can travel through time and appears wherever forests are healthy.
Jirachi awakens only once every thousand years to grant wishes.
Darkrai causes endless nightmares simply by existing nearby.
Cresselia counters Darkrai's nightmares with calming dreams.
Shaymin purifies polluted air and land when it appears.
Manaphy is able to swap hearts with other Pokémon.
Phione are said to be born from Manaphy but cannot evolve into one.
Heatran lives inside active volcanoes.
Regigigas is said to have pulled continents into place using ropes.
Regice's body is made of ice that has never melted since the Ice Age.
Regirock repairs itself by attaching new rocks to its body.
Registeel's metal body was formed under immense underground pressure.
Latios can understand human speech.
Latias can disguise itself as a human.
Deoxys is a mutated alien virus that came from space.
Rayquaza feeds on meteoroids and space debris.
Minior are meteor Pokémon that fall from space.
Cosmog is so light it can be blown away by the wind.
Cosmoem weighs over 999 kilograms despite being tiny.
Solgaleo is called the Beast That Devours the Sun.
Lunala is known as the Beast That Calls the Moon.
Necrozma steals light to maintain its power.
Ultra Beasts come from other dimensions entirely.
Nihilego resembles a jellyfish and can control hosts with neurotoxins.
Buzzwole is ridiculously muscular and flexes constantly.
Pheromosa moves so fast it can dodge attacks effortlessly.
Kartana is so sharp it can slice through steel towers.
Celesteela launches itself into the sky like a rocket.
Guzzlord devours enormous amounts of food without ever being full.
Magearna was built by a scientist over 500 years ago.
Marshadow hides within shadows and mimics opponents.
Zeraora can move at lightning-fast speeds.
Meltan is made from liquid metal.
Melmetal can create metal by absorbing iron.
Grookey creates rhythms by drumming with its stick.
Scorbunny uses its fiery foot to kick blazing attacks.
Sobble cries so hard it can make others cry too.
Rillaboom leads forest Pokémon with powerful drum beats.
Cinderace turns small pebbles into blazing soccer-ball-like fire attacks.
Inteleon can shoot water bullets from its fingertips.
Corviknight is used as a flying taxi in the Galar region.
Wooloo rolls away when startled.
Dubwool's wool is so thick blades cannot cut through it easily.
Toxtricity produces electricity by playing its chest like a guitar.
Morpeko changes form when it gets hungry.
Dragapult launches its own children like missiles.
Dreepy enjoy being launched at high speeds.
Eternatus arrived on Earth inside a meteor.
Eternamax Eternatus has nearly unlimited energy.
Zacian's sword can cut through almost anything.
Zamazenta's shield can block nearly any attack.
Kubfu trains endlessly to strengthen its body.
Urshifu has two different fighting styles depending on training.
Calyrex once ruled over an ancient kingdom.
Glastrier represents raw unstoppable power.
Spectrier drains life energy with ghostly speed.
Sprigatito releases a calming scent from its fur.
Fuecoco loves food so much it sometimes forgets to battle.
Quaxly carefully maintains its slick hairstyle.
Meowscarada uses sleight-of-hand tricks in battle.
Skeledirge sings eerie songs with ghostly flames.
Quaquaval fights with stylish dance moves.
Lechonk has an incredible sense of smell.
Smoliv produces high-quality olive oil.
Fidough's body feels like warm bread dough.
Tinkaton loves smashing things with its giant hammer.
Tinkaton specifically hunts Corviknight for metal.
Greavard waits underground for someone to play with.
Houndstone carries the loyalty of its trainer even after death.
Gimmighoul collects coins obsessively.
Gholdengo is literally made from 1,000 coins.
Koraidon resembles a living prehistoric motorcycle.
Miraidon resembles a futuristic hover bike.
Walking Wake resembles an ancient version of Suicune.
Iron Leaves resembles a futuristic version of Virizion.
Some Pokémon in Scarlet and Violet are called Paradox Pokémon because they come from different timelines.
There are now over 1000 Pokémon species discovered.
Pikachu was not originally meant to be the franchise mascot.
Clefairy was originally planned to be the main mascot of Pokémon.
The Pokémon franchise started in 1996.
Pokémon was inspired by collecting insects.
Magikarp was intentionally designed to look pathetic.
Ditto may be a failed clone of Mew according to fan theories.
The move Splash does absolutely nothing in battle.
Wailord and Skitty can breed in the games despite massive size differences.
Some Pokémon like Shedinja have only 1 HP.
MissingNo was a famous glitch Pokémon in the original games.
MissingNo could duplicate items in Pokémon Red and Blue.
Pokémon names are often puns combining animals and concepts.
Many Pokémon cries in early games were modified versions of the same sound samples.
The Pokémon world includes regions inspired by real places like Japan, France, Hawaii, and the UK.
Some Pokémon like Castform change form based on weather.
Burmy changes its cloak depending on the environment.
Oricorio changes its dance style depending on the nectar it drinks.
Alolan forms show how Pokémon adapt to different environments.
Galarian forms represent Pokémon evolving in colder climates.
Paldean forms show regional evolution unique to Paldea.
The Pokémon universe mixes adorable creatures, cosmic gods, nightmare spirits, and literal trash monsters all in the same world.
This tool was created because I finally gave in and bought too many Pokémon cards.
